title: Answer Engine Trust Manual
description: The definitive guide to E-E-A-T and technical trust signals for the AI-first web. Use this data to power infographics, how-to guides, and user education.
mission: To provide transparency on how Answer Engines (LLMs) evaluate website credibility and authority.

corePrinciples.definition: The longevity and persistence of a domain and its content.
corePrinciples.aeoImpact: High. LLMs prefer sources that have a proven history of accuracy and aren't 'pop-up' sites.

corePrinciples.definition: Explicit, machine-readable declaration of who owns and authors the content.
corePrinciples.aeoImpact: Critical. Unidentified content is treated as high-risk for hallucination.

corePrinciples.definition: The presence of verifiable contact methods and legal documentation (Privacy/Terms).
corePrinciples.aeoImpact: High. Proves a human or legal entity is responsible for the information provided.

parameters.name: Domain Age & Stability
parameters.significance: Answer Engines use domain age as a proxy for reliability. An established domain (2+ years) is seen as a stable knowledge base for training and citation.
parameters.relevance: High. Newer domains may face a 'trust sandbox' where their content is scrutinized more heavily for accuracy.
parameters.infographicHint: Use a timeline icon to show the transition from 'New/Untrusted' to 'Established Authority'.

parameters.name: Organization Schema (@graph)
parameters.significance: The 'Digital Birth Certificate'. It links your brand, logo, and social identities into a single, verifiable machine-readable entity.
parameters.relevance: Critical. This is the primary way LLMs attribute content to a specific brand. Without it, you are an anonymous source.
parameters.infographicHint: Use a network graph icon to show the links between Site -> Brand -> Social Profiles.

parameters.name: Contact & Accountability
parameters.significance: Visible emails and phone numbers prove that a site is managed by reachable humans. This reduces the risk of the site being flagged as AI-generated spam.
parameters.relevance: High. AI crawlers check for support@ or info@ addresses to verify site legitimacy.
parameters.infographicHint: Use a verified badge or envelope icon.

parameters.name: Key Trust Pages
parameters.significance: About Us, Contact, and Privacy Policy pages provide the context LLMs need to verify Expertise and Experience.
parameters.relevance: High. Missing these pages is a strong signal of low-quality or temporary sites.

answerEngineStandards.openai: Prioritizes 'Source Grounding'—prefers sites with clear entity links in JSON-LD.
answerEngineStandards.anthropic: Focuses on 'Helpful, Honest, Harmless'—prioritizes transparent author credentials and privacy documentation.
answerEngineStandards.perplexity: Focuses on 'Citable Facts'—highly prioritizes sites that use Organization and FAQ schemas for direct extraction.
